fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: The task is to open Photoshop. On the current screen, I do not see Photoshop open or in the taskbar. I need to look for Photoshop either on the desktop, in the Start menu, or in the taskbar. The Windows icon (Box ID 114) is visible, which can be used to open the Start menu and search for Photoshop.

After clicking the Windows icon, the Start menu is now open. To open Photoshop, I should search for it. There is a search bar at the top of the Start menu (Box ID 10) labeled 'Search for apps; settings; and documents'. The next step is to type 'Photoshop' in this search bar to find the application.

The Start menu search bar is now active and ready for input. To find and open Photoshop, I need to type 'Photoshop' into the search bar. This will allow the system to search for the Photoshop application so I can open it.

After typing 'Photoshop' in the Start menu search bar, the search results should display the Photoshop application. In the screenshot, the 'Adobe Photoshop' application (Box ID 12) is visible in the search results. To open Photoshop, I need to click on this result.
